summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the unsafe string concatenation in worker path matching allows path traversal sequences to escape the document root and access PHP scripts outside the intended directory.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should demonstrate that path traversal sequences in the URL path can escape the document root when processed by the vulnerable matchesPath() function, potentially allowing unauthorized PHP script execution.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully demonstrated that the unsafe concatenation of documentRoot + "/" + r.URL.Path allows path traversal sequences (../) to normalize to paths outside the document root, confirming the vulnerability exists in the code logic.
verification_method: The verification script demonstrates the path normalization behavior using the same approach as fastabs.FastAbs, showing that ../../../sensitive/secret.php can escape /var/www/html and access /var/www/sensitive/secret.php. This confirms the vulnerable code path exists and would allow unauthorized file access.
evidence_produced_by_run: Demonstrated unsafe string concatenation in workerconfig.go:181: documentRoot + "/" + r.URL.Path
Showed that path traversal sequences normalize to escape the document root
Confirmed the safe alternative (sanitizedPathJoin) exists in cgi.go but is not used in worker matching
Identified that worker scripts outside the document root would be matched and executed

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
